headline: Tech Startup Raises $200M To Build AI That Summarises Other AI Summaries
summary: Investors say the TAM is 'every human who has ever opened a Substack and immediately scrolled to the TLDR.'
fullBody: San Francisco-based startup MeghaSumm announced the close of a $200 million Series B funding round on Wednesday to build what its founders describe as 'a large language model specifically trained to summarise the outputs of other large language models.' The product, called TLDRx, ingests the summaries produced by AI assistants and compresses them further — a process the company calls 'summary distillation' and analysts are calling 'making the internet one sentence long.' Lead investor Magna Venture Partnerss said the addressable market is 'essentially every person alive who has ever opened a newsletter and immediately scrolled to the bullet points.' A demo showed TLDRx condensing a 47-page AI-generated report into the phrase 'things are fine, mostly.'
